title: Calamagrostis fibrovaginata
taxonomy: Calamagrostis fibrovaginata is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. At the genus level, it belongs to the genus Calamagrostis, which includes several species of grasses. Within the genus, C. fibrovaginata is identified by its unique characteristics and features. Its classification is a part of the broader system of plant taxonomy that organizes living organisms into hierarchical categories.

intro: Calamagrostis fibrovaginata, also known as Chinese feather-reed grass, is a perennial plant species native to China, characterized by its feathery plumes and slender stems. Traditionally, Calamagrostis fibrovaginata has been used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Calamagrostis fibrovaginata is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into formulas to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
